pattern: Once the array is sorted, fixing the smallest element of a subsequence turns the sum condition into a bound on the largest element you're allowed to pair it with, so a shrinking two-pointer window plus a running power-of-two count enumerates every valid subsequence without ever building one.
hints: Sort the array first: once sorted, the min and max of any chosen subsequence are just its leftmost and rightmost picked elements in this sorted order.

Walk two pointers inward from both ends. For the current left pointer, shrink the right pointer until nums[left] + nums[right] <= target; every element strictly between them can independently be included or excluded.

Each valid (left, right) pair with left <= right contributes 2^(right-left) subsequences (any subset of the middle elements, with nums[left] fixed as the minimum); advance left and repeat, taking care to reduce every partial sum modulo 1e9+7.

edge_inputs.description: single element equal to half the target
small mixed array from the general case
every element exactly at the minimum value 1
target too small for anything but the smallest singletons
no element by itself qualifies (min doubled exceeds target)
all elements qualify together (target covers min+max of whole array)
duplicated extremes so many subsequences share the same min/max pairing

stress.description: large random array, mid-range target, exercises the full two-pointer sweep and modulo arithmetic
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(1, min(size, 100000))
    nums = [rng.randint(1, 1000000) for _ in range(n)]
    target = rng.randint(1, 1000000)
    return [nums, target]
stress.note: n up to 1e5 with a random target; the O(n log n) sort plus O(n) sweep stays well under the time budget
